Title: Wolbachia enhances ovarian development in the rice planthopper Laodelphax striatellus through elevated energy production

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated in Wolbachia-infected nymphs and early female adults; highly expressed in midgut and Malpighian tubules; peaks on days 9 and 11 in female adults
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in Wolbachia-infected nymphs and early female adults; highly expressed in midgut and Malpighian tubules; positively correlated with Wolbachia density across tissues and developmental stages
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: riboflavin-driven ATP / energy supply supporting ovarian development
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports mitochondrial energy production and ovarian maturation, increasing fecundity
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for normal ovarian maturation; knockdown decreases emergence rate, mitochondrial complex I activity, ATP content, and mature oocyte number
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Wolbachia infection status; early post-emergence stage; dsRNA silencing; riboflavin availability
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Developmental/tissue RT-qPCR; correlation with Wolbachia density; RNAi knockdown in 5th instar nymphs; measurement of complex I activity, ATP, oocyte number, emergence and survival
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing caused high mortality during molting/emergence and reduced adult survival
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. slc52a3a is the host riboflavin transporter upregulated by Wolbachia in nymphs and early female adults. 2. Its expression positively correlates with Wolbachia density across tissues and developmental stages. 3. RNAi knockdown in 5th instar nymphs reduces emergence, complex I activity, ATP production, and mature oocyte formation, showing that riboflavin transport is required for Wolbachia-promoted ovarian development.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Silencing in 1-day or 5-day adult females had no significant effect, indicating a critical early post-emergence window.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: F1 sector beta subunit of ATP synthase
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides cellular energy for ovarian development and reproduction
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Knockdown lowers ATP content and reduces ovarian development and mature oocyte number in Wolbachia-infected females
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi knockdown followed by ATP assay, oocyte counting, and ovary morphology assessment
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ATPeF1B is important for ATP synthesis in SBPH. 2. RNAi-mediated reduction of ATPeF1B decreases ATP in newly emerged females. 3. Lower ATP leads to impaired ovarian development and fewer mature oocytes, linking host ATP synthesis to fecundity.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used mainly as functional evidence that ATP production is causally required for ovarian maturation.
